faqs.railcard: A railcard gives you 1/3 off most rail fares. Popular ones include 16-25, 26-30, and Two Together railcards.
faqs.refund: You can refund an unused ticket before departure. Advance tickets are usually non-refundable.
faqs.luggage: Most train services allow 2 pieces of hand luggage at no extra cost.
faqs.peak: Peak times are typically 7-9am and 4-7pm on weekdays. Off-peak tickets are cheaper outside these times.

rules.advance_booking: Cheapest advance tickets are released 12 weeks before travel.
rules.off_peak: Travelling off-peak or at weekends is usually cheaper.
rules.split_ticketing: Sometimes buying two tickets for parts of a journey is cheaper than one ticket for the whole journey.

ticket_types.single: A single ticket is valid for one journey in one direction.
ticket_types.return: A return ticket is valid for a round trip between two stations.
ticket_types.advance: Advance tickets are cheaper but non-refundable and only valid on specific trains.
ticket_types.off_peak: Off-peak tickets are more flexible and valid outside peak hours.
ticket_types.anytime: Anytime tickets are the most flexible but also the most expensive.

railcards.16-25: For young people aged 16-25, offering 1/3 off most rail fares.
railcards.26-30: For people aged 26-30, offering 1/3 off most rail fares.
railcards.two_together: For two named adults travelling together, offering 1/3 off most rail fares.
railcards.family: For families with children, offering 1/3 off most rail fares.
railcards.senior: For people aged 60 and over, offering 1/3 off most rail fares.
railcards.disabled: For people with disabilities, offering 1/3 off most rail fares.
